title: Pilgrimage and Governors
description: In this year, Khalid b. 'Abd al-Malik led the pilgrimage. In it, the governor of al-Madinah as well as of Mecca and al-Ta'if was Muhammad b. Hisham b. Ismail, that of Iraq and the East 456. A town, now Rabat-i-Khawja, four farsakhs east and upstream from Samarqand on the south bank of the Zarafshin River. See Barthold, T rkestan, 83, 92-3, 191; Le Strange, Lands, 465, 467. 457. Probably the Present Dargham Ariq, which separated at Waraghsar. That Asad had to do this shows that Samargand had been lost in the preceding years, perhaps in 113.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 25
